Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class WaveLens

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.MetadataWithContent
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.WaveLens
public class WaveLens extends MetadataWithContent

Java class for WaveLens complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="WaveLens">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}MetadataWithContent">
       <sequence>
         <element name="application" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="datasets" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="dateVersion" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}int" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="description" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="masterLabel" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="templateAssetSourceName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="visualizationType" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getApplication

      public String getApplication()
      Gets the value of the application property.
      Returns:
      possible object is String

    • setApplication

      public void setApplication(String value)
      Sets the value of the application property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is String

    • getDatasets

      public List<String> getDatasets()
      Gets the value of the datasets property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the datasets property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getDatasets().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String

      Returns:
      The value of the datasets property.

    • getDateVersion
